page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-22 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 22 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 11 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 176 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the c3-highmem-22 in the average tier across most standard compute and memory tests. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1720.0 and a multi-core score of 12208.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 28716.8. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1498.82 GB/sec, which drops to 106.23 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 45.62 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong performance in text generation for small (135M) language models, reaching 297.17 tokens/sec, while maintaining average speeds for larger models, such as 16.8 tokens/sec for text generation on a 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-22 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The absence of local storage and GPU hardware limits its utility for direct high-speed storage caching or heavy graphical processing, but it is well-suited for database operations, memory-intensive web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-22 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 22 dedicated vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. This instance provides 8 GB of memory per vCPU and utilizes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a Passmark CPU score of 28716.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12208.0. Notably, it exhibits strong performance in text generation for small language models at 297.17 tokens/sec. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-22 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 22 dedicated vCPUs, 176 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive workloads.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core for memory-bound applications
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector operations
Strong text generation performance for small language models at 297.17 tokens/sec
